canonical_question: When is a worker a contractor vs. employee across key jurisdictions?
aliases: ABC test vs economic reality test vs IR35
constraints: Each jurisdiction applies its own test -- no single global standard exists
US DOL suspended enforcement of 2024 independent contractor rule in May 2025; 2008 Fact Sheet #13 currently governs federal FLSA enforcement
EU Platform Workers Directive (2024/2831) must be transposed by member states by December 2, 2026 -- national implementations will vary
Australia exempts high-income contractors (above AUD 183,100 from 1 July 2025) from the whole-of-relationship test via opt-out
UK IR35 interacts with employment NICs (15% from April 2025) and Joint and Several Liability rules (April 2026) -- combined analysis required

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking only about US federal tax classification (IRS Form SS-8)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: A dedicated US-only IRS worker classification unit

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about gig economy platform-specific rules (Prop 22, app-based driver exemptions)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: A dedicated gig economy / platform worker classification unit

related_to.label: GDPR compliance (worker data processing obligations may differ for contractors vs employees)

inputs_needed.question: In which country/state is the worker performing services?
What type of work does the worker perform?
How is the worker currently engaged?
options: Through a staffing agency or intermediary
Through a personal services company (PSC) or LLC

applies_to.user_segment: Businesses engaging workers, HR/legal teams, gig platforms, staffing agencies, freelancers
applies_to.context: Determining whether a worker should be classified as an independent contractor or an employee for tax, benefits, and labor law purposes
